title: Add decoded Verilog name in JSON output (#6919)
body:
Closes https://github.com/verilator/verilator/issues/6919

I noticed there is a function `AstNode::vpiName` that does almost the same job as `AstNode::prettyName` but also wraps escaped identifiers with `\` and ` `, so I think it is the one to use.
The only difference I'm not sure about is that this function does not handle `__Viftop` in contrast to `AstNode::prettyName`.
Considering the [test](https://github.com/verilator/verilator/blob/master/test_regress/t/t_json_only_tag.py) I guess that this string is appended to some extra stuff generated for internal purposes?
(there is an instance `ifc itop()` in Verilog source but dump contains 2 entries `"type":"CELL","name":"itop","origName":"itop"` and `"type":"VAR","name":"itop","origName":"itop__Viftop"` so the second one actually does have `itop__Viftop` name).
I mean there is no much sense in creating original Verilog name for the variables that even do not exist in Verilog source as is, so this case can be ignored?
